title: Introduction
htmlOnlySample: chapter-1-introduction Welcome Welcome to MH Construction Inc.!

The purpose of this employee handbook is to briefly outline the business services provided by MH Construction Inc., provide you with a general understanding of the policies by which MH Construction Inc.
operates, and provide an outline of the procedures to be followed by each employee to maintain a smooth running, professional, and profitable operation.

title: Company Policies
htmlOnlySample: chapter-2-company-policies Equal Employment Opportunity MH Construction Inc.

shall provide Equal Employment Opportunity to all employees and applicants for employment in accordance with all applicable Equal Employment/Affirmative Action laws, directives and regulations of Federal, State, and Local governing bodies or agencies.
to foster an environment of respect for the dignity and worth of all its employees.

title: Compensation
htmlOnlySample: chapter-4-compensation and/or placed on a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP), for a period to be determined by their supervisor.
If you should decide to voluntarily leave MH Construction Inc., you are expected to provide 2 weeks notice before terminating your employment.
If you do not give this notice, your supervisor may require immediate departure.

title: Employee Benefits
htmlOnlySample: chapter-5-employee-benefits Time Keeping Policy The Federal Government requires that MH Construction Inc.
Any unauthorized employee who alters the report of another employee’s time worked will be subject to disciplinary action up to and including termination.
Disciplinary measures may include a verbal warning, written warning, disciplinary suspension, or termination of employment.

title: Miscellaneous
htmlOnlySample: chapter-6-miscellaneous Starting Jan , all employees will have premiums withheld from their paycheck to contribute to Washington Paid Family and Medical Leave fund as well as the WA cares fund.
Social Security & Medicare Through Social Security, you and your family are protected against loss of work income due to retirement, disability, or death.

Employees can contribute between 1% to 90% of their eligible compensation, either before Miscellaneous Gifts and Tips Employees are not to accept gifts, tips, gratuities, or special favors from customers or vendors.
